evidence_quotes.quote: A growing concern for millions of consumers across Europe and worldwide is the practice of video game publishers unilaterally shutting down or disabling access to gameswhether single-player or multiplayereven when consumers have paid for them.
Right to Continued Access: If a consumer has purchased a digital product, especially a single-player or peer-hosted game, they should retain access to it even after publisher support ends.
Preservation and Offline Mode: Require publishers to provide a functional offline mode or release necessary server software/tools for preservation when games are decommissioned.
Clear Consumer Rights: Establish clear guidelines that prevent companies from misleading consumers into buying games that may soon be disabled or removed.
Penalties for Anti-Consumer Practices: Include enforcement mechanisms to deter companies from destroying purchased digital goods.

paragraphs.text: I am writing to express support for the goals of the Digital Fairness Act and to request that it explicitly include provisions to protect consumers rights to access and preserve digital video games that they have legally purchased. A growing concern for millions of consumers across Europe and worldwide is the practice of video game publishers unilaterally shutting down or disabling access to gameswhether single-player or multiplayereven when consumers have paid for them. This undermines digital ownership, consumer trust, and long-term cultural preservation. I respectfully urge the Commission to consider the following in the final version of the Act: -Right to Continued Access: If a consumer has purchased a digital product, especially a single-player or peer-hosted game, they should retain access to it even after publisher support ends. -Preservation and Offline Mode: Require publishers to provide a functional offline mode or release necessary server software/tools for preservation when games are decommissioned. -Clear Consumer Rights: Establish clear guidelines that prevent companies from misleading consumers into buying games that may soon be disabled or removed. -Penalties for Anti-Consumer Practices: Include enforcement mechanisms to deter companies from destroying purchased digital goods. The Digital Fairness Act is an opportunity for the EU to lead the world in protecting digital rights, ensuring that ownership means lasting access, and not a temporary license at the publishers whim. Thank you for considering this feedback.
